item.text: I’ll answer both parts using only `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` shell commands and verify each result from tool output. First I’m going to inspect the available graph commands and then query for constructor and trait-impl sites.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I’ve confirmed `orbit.graph.search` uses `q`. Next I’m narrowing queries with kind/type filters to isolate exact Rust symbols, then I’ll walk refs/impls from those selectors.
I found the trait implementors cleanly. For `PolicyDenial`, I’m now tracing from the `LoopAuditEvent` type and then checking each reference site to separate constructors from pattern matches.

I’m testing whether the graph index exposes `PolicyDenial` through method-level search; if that’s noisy, I’ll pivot to reference-walking from `LoopAuditEvent` and inspect each candidate symbol source directly.

item.text: I confirmed `search` only keys off symbol metadata, not symbol body text. I’m now programmatically walking all `LoopAuditEvent` reference symbols and inspecting their source to pinpoint where `PolicyDenial` is actually constructed.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'refs=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.refs --input '"'{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct\"}'); printf '%s' \""'$refs" | jq -r '"'.code_refs[].selector' | while IFS= read -r sel; do out="'$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input "{'"\\\"selector\\\":\\\""'$sel'"\\\"}\"); has="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'(.source // \"\") | contains(\"PolicyDenial\")'); if [ \""'$has" = "true" ]; then file=$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.lineage[1]' | sed 's#"'^file:##'"'); lines="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.lines | @json'); name="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.selector'); printf '%s	%s	%s
' \""'$file" "$lines" "$name"; fi; done'

    SessionSpawn {
        ts: DateTime<Utc>,

        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        reason: String,
    },
    HttpRequest {
        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        provider: String,
        model: String,
        endpoint: String,
        body_sha256: String,

        ts: DateTime<Utc>,

        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        http_status: u16,
        stop_reason: String,
        usage: UsageSnapshot,

        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        tool_name: String,
        tool_use_id: String,
        input_sha256: String,
    },

        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        tool_name: String,
        tool_use_id: String,
        outcome: String,

        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        continues: bool,
    },
    PolicyDenial {
        ts: DateTime<Utc>,
        run_id: String,
        session_id: String,
        iteration: u32,
        tool_name: String,
        reason: String,
    },
}"
}

item.text:
## A. PolicyDenial constructors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: `AgentLoop::run` emits `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when a requested tool is not in the allowlist.
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` synthesizes and emits a `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when it observes a disallowed `ToolCallRequested`.

## B. AuditSink implementors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink`
